prompt:
Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition, on a clean studio white background. The reference image is the EMPTY 5ml round glass vial — reproduce its glass geometry, proportions, lighting, and shadow. Add: a navy blue cap (hex #101850) at the top in the same proportional placement as a typical 5ml capped vial, and a wraparound paper label on the front of the vial.

LABEL VERTICAL LAYOUT — EXTREME EMPHASIS: the entire text block sits VERY LOW on the label. The TOP 80 PERCENT of the label is COMPLETELY BLANK pristine white space — no text, no logos, no marks at all. ALL TEXT lives in the BOTTOM 20 PERCENT of the label. The product name 'BPC-157' is positioned in the lower portion of the label, not the middle, not the upper-middle — clearly DOWN-WEIGHTED toward the bottom of the label.

LABEL TEXT TOP TO BOTTOM (all in the bottom 20% of the label):
(1) Product name 'BPC-157' in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (~700) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered, natural letter proportions.
(2) Directly below with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line 'xx mg · 5 ml' in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (same bold weight) at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, centered, single middle-dot separator. The 'xx' must be rendered LITERALLY as two lowercase letter x characters — intentional placeholder, not a number.
(3) Below the dose with a small visual gap, two stacked compliance lines in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300, NOT bold), all-caps, medium grey, small. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'.
(4) Below with a small gap, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT, same small size, medium grey, single line, centered.
(5) At the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy accent bar at exactly hex #101850 must run UNINTERRUPTED EDGE-TO-EDGE across the full width of the label. Optional thin navy hairline above the bar.

ABSOLUTELY NO additional hairlines, no horizontal rules. Sharp focus, high resolution, premium pharmaceutical product shot, clinical minimalist aesthetic. CRITICAL: render the dose line literally as 'xx mg · 5 ml' with two lowercase x's; do NOT replace 'xx' with a number. CRITICAL POSITIONING: the upper 80% of the label MUST be empty white. The 'BPC-157' bold text must sit visibly in the lower portion of the label — close to the bottom — NOT centered or upper-middle. Treat this as a non-negotiable layout rule.
